item.title: Traffic Reforms, Outages & Dad
item.ns0:title: Traffic Reforms, Outages & Dad
item.description:
OUR NEWS: Transport Minister Outlines New Vehicle Licensing Fees and Road Traffic Modernization Plan — Saturday, June 20th, 2026
Tonight on Our News Weekend, the government unveils a major modernization of the Road Traffic Department featuring a new tiered fee structure and a transition to a cashless payment system. We also head to Eleuthera, where residents are voicing their frustrations over "unbearable" power outages that have left the island in darkness for nearly 24 hours. A former House clerk joins us to explain the historic rules of parliamentary privilege amidst ongoing legislative tension, while the Ministry of Culture reveals the symbolic new logo for the nation’s upcoming 53rd Independence celebrations. Plus, stay tuned for a special Father’s Day look at the art of making the perfect crab and rice, a vital community blood drive in Grand Bahama, and your complete holiday weekend sports and weather wrap-up.
-----
Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns1:encoded:
OUR NEWS: Transport Minister Outlines New Vehicle Licensing Fees and Road Traffic Modernization Plan — Saturday, June 20th, 2026
Tonight on Our News Weekend, the government unveils a major modernization of the Road Traffic Department featuring a new tiered fee structure and a transition to a cashless payment system. We also head to Eleuthera, where residents are voicing their frustrations over "unbearable" power outages that have left the island in darkness for nearly 24 hours. A former House clerk joins us to explain the historic rules of parliamentary privilege amidst ongoing legislative tension, while the Ministry of Culture reveals the symbolic new logo for the nation’s upcoming 53rd Independence celebrations. Plus, stay tuned for a special Father’s Day look at the art of making the perfect crab and rice, a vital community blood drive in Grand Bahama, and your complete holiday weekend sports and weather wrap-up.
-----

item.ns0:summary:
OUR NEWS: Transport Minister Outlines New Vehicle Licensing Fees and Road Traffic Modernization Plan — Saturday, June 20th, 2026
Tonight on Our News Weekend, the government unveils a major modernization of the Road Traffic Department featuring a new tiered fee structure and a transition to a cashless payment system. We also head to Eleuthera, where residents are voicing their frustrations over "unbearable" power outages that have left the island in darkness for nearly 24 hours. A former House clerk joins us to explain the historic rules of parliamentary privilege amidst ongoing legislative tension, while the Ministry of Culture reveals the symbolic new logo for the nation’s upcoming 53rd Independence celebrations. Plus, stay tuned for a special Father’s Day look at the art of making the perfect crab and rice, a vital community blood drive in Grand Bahama, and your complete holiday weekend sports and weather wrap-up.
-----

item.ns0:author: Our News Bahamas
